title: Meyniel's Conjecture
statement_text: The cop number of any connected graph on $n$ vertices can be bounded by $O(\sqrt{n})$.
context_text: Meyniel's conjecture is described as one of the main open questions in the Cops and Robbers area. The paper improves cop number upper bounds for graphs of bounded diameter, obtaining $c(G) < n^{3/5+o(1)}$ for diameter at most 4 and $c(G) < n^{3/7+o(1)}$ for diameter at most 3, both of which are improvements over previously known bounds and constitute partial progress toward Meyniel's conjecture for these graph classes.
paper_contribution: Proves improved cop number upper bounds for graphs of bounded diameter: $c(G) < n^{3/5+o(1)}$ when the diameter is at most 4 (improving the prior bound of $n^{2/3+o(1)}$) and $c(G) < n^{3/7+o(1)}$ when the diameter is at most 3 (improving the prior bound of $n^{3/5+o(1)}$), using a novel 'real and imaginary cops' strategy.
notes: The PDF extraction is truncated after the diameter-3 argument (the text breaks off mid-sentence and is followed by a long list of arXiv IDs), so any additional conjectures or open problems stated later in the paper (e.g., in a concluding section) are not captured here.
paper_title: Cops and Robbers on graphs of bounded diameter
